---
title: "Untitled"
format:
  html:
    embed-resources: true
    toc: true
editor: visual
---

```{r}
#| message: false
library('tidyverse')
```

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Data Preprocessing

```{r}
tplant <- read_csv(
  file = "data/tplant_observations - main_2025-04-09.csv",
  na = c("Na", "n", "na"),
  col_types = cols(
    ID = col_character(),
    Tray = col_factor(),
    Row = col_integer(),
    Column = col_integer(),
    n_shoot_axis = col_integer(),
    Leaf_shape = col_factor(),
    Leaf_edge = col_factor(),
    Comments = col_character()
  )
) |>
  filter(!(is.na(ID))) |> # remove plants without ID/label
  mutate(
    # change pop 165 to 156
    ID = case_when(ID == "165-1" ~ "156-1",
                   ID == "165-2" ~ "156-2",
                   ID == "165-3" ~ "156-3",
                   TRUE ~ ID),
    Pop = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "^.*(?=-)"),
    # extract Population number from the ID
    Replicate = stringi::stri_extract(ID, regex = "(?<=-).*$"),
    # extract Replicate number from the ID
    Leaf_shape = case_when(Leaf_shape == "o" ~ "O", Leaf_shape == "l" ~ "L", TRUE ~ Leaf_shape),
    Leaf_edge = case_when(Leaf_edge == "s" ~ "S", Leaf_edge == "g" ~ "G", TRUE ~ Leaf_edge)
  ) |>
  select(ID, Pop, Replicate, everything()) 
```

```{r}
ID_is_not_alive_A_tha <- c(
  "384-20",
  "486-7",
  "463-5",
  "384-12",
  "346-15",
  "384-15",
  "472-6",
  "408-5",
  "408-9",
  "346-16",
  "482-5",
  "247-1",
  "465-1",
  "409-12",
  "409-18",
  "409-19",
  "409-5",
  "409-14",
  "494-18",
  "406-15",
  "406-17",
  "438-9",
  "466-21",
  "346-14",
  "463-1",
  "345-7",
  "471-8",
  "452-5",
  "406-18",
  "452-8",
  "345-10",
  "342-13",
  "406-19",
  "406-20",
  "342-12",
  "345-20",
  "159-5",
  "459-13",
  "451-16",
  "375-1",
  "471-9",
  "475-3",
  "45-18",
  "471-5",
  "344-10",
  "471-6",
  "454-1",
  "7-5",
  "375-5",
  "487-10",
  "479-20",
  "468-1",
  "375-4",
  "442-4",
  "408-8",
  "346-8",
  "409-13",
  "375-2",
  "346-12",
  "480-10",
  "346-13",
  "409-6",
  "409-20",
  "205-1",
  "470-3",
  "34-16",
  "176-2",
  "360-1",
  "160-2",
  "345-4",
  "345-13",
  "472-13",
  "345-18",
  "213-6",
  "494-19",
  "486-16",
  "494-17",
  "347-17",
  "104-9",
  "213-4",
  "408-7",
  "205-5",
  "184-1",
  "187-2",
  "433-1",
  "475-6",
  "409-10",
  "494-16",
  "409-7",
  "180-8",
  "488-19",
  "409-9",
  "409-8",
  "409-17",
  "267-21",
  "407-14",
  "465-2",
  "468-2",
  "494-7",
  "100-1",
  "348-18",
  "7-17",
  "468-15",
  "491-4",
  "406-10",
  "493-1"
)

Pop_pool_sampled <- c("1", "7", "8", "24", "27", "34", "35", "41", "45", "47", "49", "102", "104", "105", "113", "122", "159", "168", "169", "172", "173", "177", "180", "183", "184", "203", "232", "233", "258", "263", "264", "265", "267", "282", "310", "342", "344", "345", "346", "347", "348", "357", "359", "384", "405", "406", "408", "428", "433", "438", "439", "447", "448", "449", "451", "453", "458", "459", "466", "468", "472", "474", "477", "478", "479", "480", "481", "482", "483", "484", "486", "487", "488", "489", "491", "494")

Pop_indv_sampled <- c("19", "24", "29", "41", "45", "94", "105", "106", "113", "120", "122", "161", "167", "177", "182", "187", "191", "205", "206", "213", "233", "241", "255", "264", "357", "359", "361", "375", "407", "409", "424", "429", "438", "441", "444", "450", "452", "454", "456", "462", "463", "469", "470", "471", "475", "476")
# Pop 409 changed from Pool to individual sampled, sampled 1, 2, 3, 4, 11; Pop 407 changed from Pool to individual

#ID_skipped_sampling <- c("172-5", "180-3", "180-4", "180-7", "180-11", "184-1", "184-5", "122-18", "122-20", "408-7", "346-16", "408-7", "120-2", "182-4", "205-1", "205-5", "24-5", "24-12", "24-18", "24-20", "1-13", "7-5", "7-8", "45-8", "45-10", "45-11", "45-12", "45-20", "159-3", "159-4", "159-9", "159-10", "159-13", "168-12", "168-14", "168-16", "177-4", "177-8", "177-9", "177-10", "177-12", "177-13", "177-14", "177-16", "177-18", "177-20", "233-7", "468-12", "408-3")

# the column sampletype shows the assigned type of sampling per population, but
# gives no information if the replicate was sampled or not
tplant <- tplant |>
  mutate(is_alive_A_tha = case_when(!(ID %in% ID_is_not_alive_A_tha) ~ "TRUE", TRUE ~ "FALSE")) |>
  mutate(
    sampletype = case_when(
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Pool_and_Indv",
      Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled &
        !(Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled) ~ "Pool",
      !(Pop %in% Pop_pool_sampled) &
        Pop %in% Pop_indv_sampled ~ "Indv",
      TRUE ~ "Not_sampled"
    )
  )
  
```

```{r}
# Summing up replicates for each population
summed_pop <- tplant |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  summarise(total_repl = n()) |>
  arrange(desc(total_repl))

summed_pop
```

```{r}
# add label for threshold of total replicats above 5
summed_pop_above_3 <- summed_pop |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl > 3, T, F))

summed_pop_above_3
```

```{r}
summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T) |>
  nrow()
```

Out of the 164 populations 110 have more than 3 replicates, 54 less.

The following Population have not 4 replicates.

```{r}
# Populations below threshold of replicates
tplant_pop_below_3 <- summed_pop_above_3 |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == F) 

tplant_pop_below_3$Pop
```

```{r}
# Add the above to the tplant df
tplant <- tplant |>
  group_by(Pop) |>
  filter(is_alive_A_tha == TRUE)|>
  mutate(total_repl_in_pop = n()) |>
  ungroup() |>
  mutate(pop_above_3 = ifelse(total_repl_in_pop > 3, T, F)) |>
  relocate(Comments, .after = last_col())


tplant
```

```{r}
# Add the ecosystem type to the tplant df
Phenology <- read_csv(file = "data/Phenology_field_2.csv",
                               col_types = cols(
                                 Site_ID = col_character(),
                                 Type = col_factor(),
                                 First_FT_julianday = col_double()
                               )) |>
  select(Site_ID, Type)

tplant <- tplant |>
  left_join(Phenology, join_by("Pop" == "Site_ID")) |>
  mutate(
    Type = case_when(
      Type == "wall" ~ "Wall",
      Type == "grass" ~ "Grass",
      Type == "pavement" ~ "Pavement",
      TRUE ~ Type
    )
  )
```

## EDA of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge per Environment

```{r}
Ecotype_colors <- c(
  "wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "grass" = "#6a176e",
  "pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)

# Hist pop per eco type
pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = TRUE) |>
  select(Pop, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_pop_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_pop_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Number of Populations per Type")
```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
repl_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(Replicate, Type) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_repl_per_eco = n())

ggplot(data = repl_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_repl_per_eco, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Total Individuals of the Populations by Type")
```

```{r}
leaf_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_shape) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_shape_long <- leaf_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_L, prop_O),
    names_to = "Leaf_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.8)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_L" = "#6a176e", "prop_O" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Elongated", "Oval")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_)

leaf_edge_long <- leaf_edge_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_G, prop_S),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_G" = "#6a176e", "prop_S" = "#f8cd37"),
    labels = c("Smooth", "Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_shape_proportions <- tplant |> 
  drop_na(Leaf_edge, Leaf_shape) |>
  mutate(Leaf_edge_shape = paste0(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge)) |>
  count(Type, Leaf_edge_shape, name = "count") |> 
  group_by(Type) |> 
  mutate(proportion = round(count / sum(count), 2)) |> 
  select(-count) |> 
  pivot_wider(
    names_from = Leaf_edge_shape, 
    values_from = proportion,
    names_prefix = "prop_",  
    values_fill = 0
  ) |> 
  ungroup() |>
  select(-prop_, -prop_Lx)

leaf_edge_shape_long <- leaf_edge_shape_proportions |> 
  pivot_longer(
    cols = c(prop_LG, prop_LS, prop_OG, prop_OS),
    names_to = "Leaf_edge_shape",
    values_to = "Proportion"
  )

ggplot(leaf_edge_shape_long, 
       aes(x = Type, y = Proportion, fill = Leaf_edge_shape)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = position_dodge(width = 0.9)) +
  scale_fill_manual(
    values = c("prop_LG" = "#6a176e", "prop_LS" = "#f8cd37",
               "prop_OG" = "#bc3754", "prop_OS" = "#f37819"),
    labels = c("Long Smooth", "Long Serrated", "Oval Smooth", "Oval Serrated")
  ) +
  labs(x = "Type", y = "Proportion", fill = "Leaf Edge Shape") +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "top",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  )



```

```{r}
# Hist replicates per eco type
axis_per_eco <- tplant |>
  select(n_shoot_axis, Type) |>
  drop_na(Type, n_shoot_axis) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(mean_axis = round(mean(n_shoot_axis), 1))

ggplot(data = axis_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = mean_axis, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Average Shoot Axis per Type")
```

```{r}
# distribution of sampled pop across ecotpy?
dist_sampled_pop_per_eco <- tplant |>
  filter(pop_above_3 == T,
         sampletype != "Not_sampled") |>
  distinct(Pop, .keep_all = T) |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  summarise(n_sampled_pop = n() )

ggplot(data = dist_sampled_pop_per_eco,
       mapping = aes(y = n_sampled_pop, x = Type, fill = Type)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  scale_fill_manual(values = c(
  "Wall" = "#f8cd37",
  "Grass" = "#6a176e",
  "Pavement" = "#bc3754",
  "Tree bed" = "#f37819"
)) +
  theme_minimal() +
  theme(
    legend.position = "none",
    axis.text.x = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman"),
    axis.title.y = element_text(size = 25, family = "Times New Roman")
  ) +
  labs(y = "Populations with Leafs Sampled from")
```

## First Statistical analysis of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge per Environment

Since the \~2000 plants are coming from Populations, individuals from the same Population are more similar to each other, creating non-independence. To address this a proportion of the type of leaf edge and shape is calculated per population.

```{r}
prop_trait_per_pop <- tplant |>
  drop_na(Leaf_shape, Leaf_edge) |>
  group_by(Pop, Type, total_repl_in_pop) |> # group by Pop but include Type and total_repl_in_pop in sum table
  summarise(
    n_L = sum(Leaf_shape == "L"),
    n_O = sum(Leaf_shape == "O"),
    n_S = sum(Leaf_edge == "S"),
    n_G = sum(Leaf_edge == "G"),
    
    # avoid division by 0 by adding 0.5
    log_ratio_L_O = log((n_L + 0.5) / (n_O + 0.5)),
    # zero means same proportion, positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval leaf shape (O)
    log_ratio_S_G = log((n_S + 0.5) / (n_G + 0.5)),
  ) |>
  mutate(Type = as.factor(Type)) 

# bad results, perhaps because of uneven pop per envs
# leaf_shape_model <- glm(log_ratio_L_O ~ Type,
#                         data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#                         family = gaussian())
# summary.lm(leaf_shape_model)
# # R**2 = 3%, not sign.
# 
# leaf_edge_model <- glm(log_ratio_S_G ~ Type,
#                        data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#                        family = gaussian())
# summary.lm(leaf_edge_model)
# # R**2 6%, sign.
# 
# trait_interaction <- glm(log_ratio_S_G ~ log_ratio_L_O * Type,
#                         family = gaussian(),
#                         data = prop_trait_per_pop)
# summary.lm(trait_interaction)

# create balanced dataset through random subsampling
min_pop_per_type <- min(table(prop_trait_per_pop$Type))

# table(prop_trait_per_pop$Type)
# 
#    Grass Pavement Tree bed     Wall 
#       21       53       30       42 

set.seed(123)
balanced_prop_trait_per_pop <- prop_trait_per_pop |>
  group_by(Type) |>
  sample_n(min_pop_per_type) |>
  ungroup()
```

The number of Populations where evened by random subsampling to the lowest number of population per Type.

```{r}
leaf_shape_by_type_model <- glm(log_ratio_L_O ~ Type,
                        data = balanced_prop_trait_per_pop,
                        family = gaussian())
summary(leaf_shape_by_type_model)
# positive number means more elongated leaf shape (L), negative oval

# genetic drift?
# leaf_shape_by_total_repl_in_pop_model <- glm(log_ratio_L_O ~ total_repl_in_pop,
#                         data = balanced_prop_trait_per_pop,
#                         family = gaussian())
# summary(leaf_shape_by_total_repl_in_pop_model)
```



```{r}
ggplot(balanced_prop_trait_per_pop, aes(x = Type, y = log_ratio_L_O, color = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    y = "Log Ratio (Elongated/Rounded Leaves)",
    x = "Environment Type"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```

```{r}
leaf_edge_model <- glm(log_ratio_S_G ~ Type,
                       data = balanced_prop_trait_per_pop,
                       family = gaussian())
summary(leaf_edge_model)
```

The transplanted plants showed a significant difference in leaf edge by environment (Adjusted R-squared: 0.068, F\_{3,80} = 3.009, p-value = 0.035). Plants that lived in the `Wall` environment showed significantly less serrated leafs.

```{r}
trait_interaction <- glm(log_ratio_S_G ~ log_ratio_L_O * Type,
                        family = gaussian(),
                        data = balanced_prop_trait_per_pop)
summary(trait_interaction)
```

```{r}
ggplot(balanced_prop_trait_per_pop, aes(x = Type, y = log_ratio_S_G, color = Type)) +
  geom_boxplot(alpha = 0.7) +
  geom_jitter(width = 0.2, alpha = 0.4, size = 2) +
  geom_hline(yintercept = 0, linetype = "dashed") +
  labs(
    y = "Log Ratio (Serrated/Not Serrated Leaves)",
    x = "Environment Type"
  ) +
  theme_classic() +
  theme(legend.position = "none")
```



## Second attempt at Statistical analysis of Leaf Shape and Leaf Edge per Environment

```{r}
# Create weights based on the size of the populations to take into effect that about one third of 
# the populations have 3 or lss replicates
# Create Categories of Trait and Trait combo per pop
trait_per_pop <- prop_trait_per_pop |>
  mutate(
    pop_weights = total_repl_in_pop / 25,
    leaf_shape_catg = if_else(log_ratio_L_O > 0, "Elongated", "Rounded", missing = NA),
    leaf_edge_catg = if_else(log_ratio_S_G > 0, "Serrated", "Not_Serrated", missing = NA),
    trait_combo = paste0(leaf_shape_catg, "_", leaf_edge_catg)
  )
```

```{r}
ggplot(trait_per_pop, aes(x = n_O, y = n_L, color = Type, shape = leaf_edge_catg)) +
  geom_jitter() +
  theme_minimal() +
  labs(
    y = "Number of Elongated Leafs per Population",
    x = "Number of Oval Leafs per Population",
  )
```

```{r}
library(lme4)
library(car)
library(multcomp)

# Does the type of environment effect the leaf shape of rosette leafs of A. thaliana populations, while taking into effect the uneven distribution of Populations over the types of environment and the difference in size of population?
shape_model1 <- glmer(
  cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (1 | Pop), # Pop uneven distributed across types of env
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  weights = trait_per_pop$pop_weights, # total_repl_in_pop / 40,
  data = prop_trait_per_pop,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
)
# AIC decreases with threshold increasing, meaning that the model predictions get more accurate when
# giving larger populations more weight e.g. pop_weights = pmin(total_repl_in_pop, 40) / 40,
  
# shape_model2 <- glmer(cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (1|Pop), 
#                         family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#                         data = prop_trait_per_pop)
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model2)
# shape_model1 is better than 2

# shape_model3 <- glm(cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type, 
#                     family = quasibinomial(link = "logit"),
#                     data = prop_trait_per_pop)
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model3)
# 
# summary(shape_model3)
# 
# shape_model4 <- glmer(cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (1|Pop), 
#                     family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#                     data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#                     control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa"))
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model4)

# shape_model5 <- glmer(
#   cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + scale(total_repl_in_pop) + (1 | Pop),
#   family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#   data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
# )
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model5)

# shape_model6 <- glmer(
#   cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (1 | Pop / total_repl_in_pop),
#   family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#   data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
# )
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model6)

# shape_model7 <- glmer(
#   cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (0 + factor(total_repl_in_pop) | Pop),
#   family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#   data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
# )
# 
# AIC(shape_model1, shape_model7)

# shape_model8 <- glmer(
#   cbind(n_L, n_O) ~ Type + (1 | Pop), # Pop uneven distributed across types of env
#   family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#   weights = trait_per_pop$pop_max_weights, # pop_max_weights = total_repl_in_pop / max(total_repl_in_pop),
#   data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
# )
# performs worse than with capped threshold. Larger populations give more information but a
# more compressed scale is better for the model?



overdisp <- sum(residuals(shape_model1, type="pearson")^2) / df.residual(shape_model1)
cat("overdispersion: ", overdisp, "\n")  # Should be close to 1 if no overdispersion

summary(shape_model1)
Anova(shape_model1, type = "II")
summary(glht(shape_model1,linfct = mcp(Type="Tukey")))
```

Low AIC of 183.6, and underdispersion. Weighing larger Populations in stronger increases the model, but it is capped at 40.

```{r}
# Does the type of environment effect the leaf edge of rosette leafs of A. thaliana populations, while taking into effect the uneven distribution of Populations over the types of environment and the difference in size of population?
edge_model1 <- glmer(
  cbind(n_S, n_G) ~ Type + (1 | Pop), # Pop uneven distributed across types of env
  family = binomial(link = "logit"),
  weights = trait_per_pop$pop_weights, # total_repl_in_pop / 40,
  data = prop_trait_per_pop,
  control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
)
# AIC decreases with threshold increasing, meaning that the model predictions get more accurate when
# giving larger populations more weight e.g. pop_weights = pmin(total_repl_in_pop, 40) / 40,
  


overdisp <- sum(residuals(edge_model1, type="pearson")^2) / df.residual(edge_model1)
cat("overdispersion: ", overdisp)  # Should be close to 1 if no overdispersion

summary(edge_model1)
Anova(edge_model1, type = "II")
summary(glht(edge_model1,linfct = mcp(Type="Tukey")))
```

```{r}
# # Test for interaction between traits
# model_interaction <- glmer(cbind(n_S, n_G) ~ log_ratio_L_O + (1|Pop),
#                            family = binomial(link = "logit"),
#   weights = trait_per_pop$pop_weights, # total_repl_in_pop / 40,
#   data = prop_trait_per_pop,
#   control = glmerControl(optimizer = "bobyqa")
#   )
# 
# overdisp <- sum(residuals(model_interaction, type="pearson")^2) / df.residual(model_interaction)
# overdisp  # Should be close to 1 if no overdispersion
# 
# summary(model_interaction)
# Anova(model_interaction, type = "II")
# summary(glht(model_interaction,linfct = mcp(Type="Tukey")))

# maybe too complex for binomial model
```

```{r}
cor.test(trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O, trait_per_pop$log_ratio_S_G)
```

Traits positive correlated.
### Account for multiple testing
```{r}
# p-value from main hypothesis
shape_type_pvalue <- Anova(shape_model1, type = "II")["Type", "Pr(>Chisq)"]
edge_type_pvalue <- Anova(edge_model1, type = "II")["Type", "Pr(>Chisq)"]
correlation_pvalue <- cor.test(trait_per_pop$log_ratio_L_O, trait_per_pop$log_ratio_S_G)$p.value

all_pvalues <- c(shape_type_pvalue, edge_type_pvalue, correlation_pvalue)

adjusted_pvalues_bonf <- p.adjust(all_pvalues, method = "bonferroni")

results_df <- data.frame(
  Test = c("Shape ~ Type", "Edge ~ Type", "Shape-Edge Correlation"),
  Original_p = all_pvalues,
  Bonferroni_p = adjusted_pvalues_bonf
) |>
  mutate(Significant = case_when(
    Bonferroni_p < 0.05 ~ "Significant",
    Bonferroni_p > 0.05 ~ "N.S.",
  ))
print(results_df)
```
